calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-01-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters are heard concurrently:
CGC-09-275391 Margarita Flores, et al. VS. Asbestos Defendants.
CGC-11-275860 Dominic Rielli VS. Certaineed Corporation.

APPEARANCES:
Richard M. Grant of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
K. Albert Loquaci (415-890-5252) and Ethan H. Seibert (415-890-5184) of Nicolaides Fink Thorpe...LLP appearing for Specially Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company.

RULING:
Hearing held, ARGUED. Moving party states they have not denied coverage, is continuing investigation of coverage, and requests court to take notice of 11 exhibits. The Court GRANTS the request for Judicial Notice. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.)

Order signed in chambers.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Court Reporter: Nancy E. Presant, CSR 906 (Tel: 510-757-8550; email: npresantcsr@gmail.com). (503/CML)

Off Calendar Aug-06-2015 Continued To Feb-04-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon , CSR#6840.

Simon S. Grille, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs, MARGARITA FLORES, as wrongful Death Heir, and as Successor-in Interest to ANTONIO FLORES, deceased; and YOLANDA FLORES, SANDRA DELAO, LYDIA FLORES and SEAN CANO, by and Thru His Guardian Ad Litem, MARGARITA FLORES, as Legal Heirs of ANTONIO FLORESE, deceased ("plaintiffs").
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $82,339.00 in Economic Damages, $1,200,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $1,282,339.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

Minor'S Compromise
Off Calendar Sep-29-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Brenda Fields, #7235.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq. appeared for and with the petitioner Margarita Flores, the Guardian ad Litem for Sean Cano, a minor claimant.
On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved.
Settlement of $5,000.00
Attorney's fees $1,190.54.
Costs $237.86
Net Balance $3, 571.60 to be deposit into a blocked account with US Bank, 659 Main St., Martinez, CA. 94553-1128.
Orders signed in open court.
